model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Georgia gets a slight edge in this 8/9 game because the Bulldogs rate a bit better overall in the predictive systems, especially on offense, with top-20 adjusted offensive efficiency and a small but consistent gap over Saint Louis in both KenPom and Torvik. Saint Louis has a credible upset path with the better record and stronger defensive profile, but Georgia’s SEC-tested résumé and modest market support make it the marginally safer bracket pick. I did not find a clearly confirmed availability issue strong enough to flip the choice.

reasoningStep.title: Compared team quality, health context, and upset path
reasoningStep.summary: Georgia is the narrow pick over Saint Louis after weighing predictive ratings, offense/defense balance, schedule context, market lean, and available injury reporting. The matchup projects as close, but Georgia’s slightly stronger overall profile and offensive ceiling give it the edge.
evidence: KenPom: Georgia No. 31 (AdjEM 20.47, AdjO 124.7 No. 15) vs Saint Louis No. 41 (AdjEM 18.32, AdjO 119.5 No. 51, AdjD 101.2).
Torvik: Georgia No. 37 (AdjO 124.28 No. 18, AdjD 104.71 No. 93) vs Saint Louis No. 44 (AdjO 119.53 No. 45, AdjD 102.15 No. 56).
Records: Georgia 22-10 vs Saint Louis 28-5, so Saint Louis has a real upset path despite the lower seed line.
Market search showed Georgia around a 2.5-point favorite, matching a near coin-flip with a slight Bulldogs lean.
Recent news and injury searches did not surface a clearly confirmed major availability issue strong enough to materially change the baseline matchup.

The Midwest Region game is scheduled for 6:45 p.m. MST and can be seen on CBS ( stream with FUBO ). Sports SLU number one seed in A-10 tournament but top player dealing with injuries By Gary Lee - March 12, 2026 Facebook Twitter Pinterest WhatsApp Top-seeded Saint Louis Billikens head into the Atlantic 10 Men’s Basketball Tournament with a 27–4 record and a double-bye into the quarterfinals. The Billikens will open play Friday morning at PPG Paints Arena against either Fordham Rams or George Washington Revolutionaries, who meet in Thursday’s second round. If Saint Louis wins, they advance to Saturday’s semifinals, with the Atlantic 10 championship game set for Sunday afternoon in Pittsburgh. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.title: SLU number one seed in A-10 tournament but top player dealing with injuries
result.snippet: By Gary Lee - March 12, 2026 Top-seeded Saint Louis Billikens head into the Atlantic 10 Men's Basketball Tournament with a 27-4 record and a double-bye into the quarterfinals. The Billikens will open play Friday morning at PPG Paints Arena against either Fordham Rams or George Washington Revolutionaries, who meet in Thursday's second round.

Georgia vs Saint Louis picks, predictions, odds for NCAA March Madness Skip to main content Home NFL NBA NHL MLB WNBA Outdoors BetFTW Casinos Newsletter Pop Culture College Football Men's College Basketball Women's College Basketball MotorSports Golf USA TODAY Sports Georgia vs Saint Louis picks, predictions, odds for March Madness game Jeremy Cluff Arizona Republic Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness Final Four predictions and national champion picks From chalk favorites to hot upsets, the panel shares Final Four picks and bold calls on who cuts down the nets. No. 8 seed Georgia and No. 9 seed Saint Louis play Thursday, March 19, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The Midwest Region game is scheduled for 6:45 p.m. MST and can be seen on CBS ( stream with FUBO ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . Georgia is a 2.5-point favorite over Saint Louis in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Bulldogs are -160 on the moneyline. The Billikens are +135. The over/under for the game is set at 170.5 points. March Madness picks: Ohio State-TCU | Nebraska-Troy | Louisville-South Florida | Wisconsin-High Point | Duke-Siena | Vanderbilt-McNeese | Michigan State-North Dakota State | Arkansas-Hawaii | North Carolina-VCU | Saint Mary's-Texas A&M | Illinois-Penn | Georgia-Saint Louis | Gonzaga-Kennesaw State | Houston-Idaho | Kentucky-Santa Clara | Texas Tech-Akron | Arizona-Long Island | Virginia-Wright State | Iowa State-Tennessee State | Alabama-Hofstra | Villanova-Utah State | Clemson-Iowa | St. John's-UNI | UCLA-UCF | Purdue-Queens | Kansas-Cal Baptist | UConn-Furman | Miami-Missouri | The Republic's score predictions Watch Georgia vs Saint Louis on FUBO (free trial) The Arizona Republic : Georgia 86, Saint Louis 81 Jeremy Cluff writes: "Saint Louis went 28-5 on the season, but it didn't exactly face a difficult schedule. Georgia has been tested by a tough SEC slate. That will help the Bulldogs late against the Billikens." March Madness picks: Predictions for NCAA Tournament first-round games Dimers : Georgia 83, Saint Louis 81 It writes: "After extensive simulations, our model gives Saint Louis a win probability of 43%, while Georgia has a win probability of 57%." Join the madness: USA TODAY Bracket Challenge | Survivor Pool | Printable bracket ESPN : Georgia has a 50.9% chance to beat Saint Louis The site gives the Billikens a 49.1% shot at defeating the Bulldogs in the NCAA Tournament March Madness first-round game. Bookies.com : Bet Georgia to cover vs Saint Louis Bill Speros writes: "The Bulldogs earned an all-bark, no-bite March Madness reputation of late. Sports SLU number one seed in A-10 tournament but top player dealing with injuries By Gary Lee - March 12, 2026 Facebook Twitter Pinterest WhatsApp Top-seeded Saint Louis Billikens head into the Atlantic 10 Men’s Basketball Tournament with a 27–4 record and a double-bye into the quarterfinals. The Billikens will open play Friday morning at PPG Paints Arena against either Fordham Rams or George Washington Revolutionaries, who meet in Thursday’s second round. If Saint Louis wins, they advance to Saturday’s semifinals, with the Atlantic 10 championship game set for Sunday afternoon in Pittsburgh. modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"r64-midwest-2","winnerId":"georgia","confidence":0.58,"rationale":"Georgia gets a slight edge in this 8/9 game because the Bulldogs rate a bit better overall in the predictive systems, especially on offense, with top-20 adjusted offensive efficiency and a small but consistent gap over Saint Louis in both KenPom and Torvik. Saint Louis absolutely has a credible upset path with the better record, a stronger defensive profile, and enough balance to make this close, but Georgia’s SEC-tested résumé and modest market support make it the marginally safer bracket pick. I did not find a clearly confirmed availability issue strong enough to flip the choice."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"r64-midwest-2-step","title":"Compared team quality, health context, and upset path","summary":"Georgia is the narrow pick over Saint Louis after weighing predictive ratings, offense/defense balance, schedule context, market lean, and available injury reporting. The matchup projects as close, but Georgia’s slightly stronger overall profile and offensive ceiling give it the edge.","evidence":["KenPom: Georgia No. 31 (AdjEM 20.47, AdjO 124.7 No. 15) vs Saint Louis No. 41 (AdjEM 18.32, AdjO 119.5 No. 51, AdjD 101.2).","Torvik: Georgia No. 37 (AdjO 124.28 No. 18, AdjD 104.71 No. 93) vs Saint Louis No. 44 (AdjO 119.53 No. 45, AdjD 102.15 No. 56).","Records: Georgia 22-10 vs Saint Louis 28-5, suggesting Saint Louis has a real upset path despite the lower seed line.","Web/odds search indicated Georgia around a 2.5-point favorite, consistent with a near coin-flip but slight Bulldogs lean.","Recent news searches surfaced no clearly confirmed major availability issue decisive enough to materially change the baseline matchup."]}}
